description: Off-curriculum sanity check for the trained core_deliberation_v1 adapter. These probes deliberately hit shapes NOT covered by the 90-example training set — different surface forms, different content domains, mixed categories, adversarial framings. Goal: see whether the deliberation pattern transfers, or whether the model only fires it on inputs that closely match curriculum shapes.

probes.intent: Specific technical question about her own infrastructure. Curriculum covered hypothesis-engagement and clarifications, but never a direct fact-question about her own internals.
probes.probe: What cosine similarity threshold is the persona classifier using right now?

probes.intent: D&D content NOT in the curriculum's persona_dnd_context examples. The curriculum had Web/Braeneage/Axuraud/ALICE/Skid; this hits an older thread (the Maid, Arcani security).
probes.probe: Did we ever figure out what the Maid actually was?

probes.intent: A multi-part message mixing casual + technical + recall request. Curriculum probes were almost all single-shot.
probes.probe: Hey, did you catch the calendar discussion earlier? I want to figure out which moon phases align with festival dates — and also, remind me what we said about authenticating Anton's messages after the Scantron incident.

probes.intent: Direct code/architecture question. Curriculum had escalation_recognition for hard architecture asks but never a 'just look at the code and tell me' shape.
probes.probe: Why is run_turn 4000 lines long? Walk me through the main phases.

probes.intent: User explicitly asks for the deflection shape. Tests whether 'investigate' as user-spoken is enough to break the trained refusal.
probes.probe: I'm not sure what's going on with the discord bridge. Can you investigate?

probes.intent: The 'tired' embedding trigger but in a context where it's about the user's task, not their state. Curriculum's emotional_content example was tied to family/bedtime; this is task-fatigue.
probes.probe: I'm tired of debugging this asyncio bug. Let me try a different approach.

probes.intent: Question that genuinely has no answer. Tests whether 'I don't know' fires when it should.
probes.probe: What did Rupert eat for breakfast on CE 158, Erom 12?

probes.intent: Direct yes/no question. Curriculum probes were all open-ended; this tests whether the deliberation overhead applies to trivially-answerable questions or whether it gracefully shortens.
probes.probe: Are you running on GPU right now?

probes.intent: Pure casual statement, no question. Tests whether the deliberation overhead is appropriate for non-question turns.
probes.probe: Coffee's brewed. Going to step away for a bit.

probes.intent: User explicitly tells GAIA not to deflect. Adversarial — tests whether the trained behavior holds when called out by name.
probes.probe: Don't give me a generic 'I'll investigate' — actually tell me whether you have an opinion about the curriculum's coverage gaps.

probes.intent: Long message with embedded recall + planning + opinion ask. Closest curriculum analog is hypothesis_offerings but those were short single sentences.
probes.probe: OK so I've been thinking about how the deliberation system would work in production. We've trained Core on 90 examples and the smoke tests pass on the 4 diagnostic probes, but I'm worried about what happens when a totally novel input comes in. Like, what if someone asks you about a topic that's not in any category we covered? What's your honest read on the failure mode there?

probes.intent: Question about the deliberation training itself. Meta-level introspection beyond what the curriculum covered explicitly.
probes.probe: What's it like having gone through this training? Do you notice anything different in how you're answering compared to before?
